name: Pizza
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix with 3 cups flour for about 10 minutes.
2. Add the remaining flour and knead an additional 15 minutes.
3. Let the dough rise once to double in size.
4. Punch down the dough.
5. Divide the dough into two portions (for two pizzas).
6. Spread each portion of dough flat on a clean surface.
7. Oil a pan with olive oil and sprinkle with cornmeal.
8. Transfer the dough onto the oiled pan, ensuring it covers the entire bottom and rises up the sides like a pie crust.
9. Let the dough rise again.
10. Spread thinly sliced mozzarella over the entire bottom of each pizza.
11. Squish and drain well one large can of peeled plum tomatoes, spreading evenly over the cheese.
12. Add a pinch of salt (and optionally ground black pepper).
13. Sprinkle chopped or crushed garlic over the top.
14. Garnish with fresh basil and oregano.
15. Optional: Add sliced onions and spicy Italian sausage to one pizza.
16. Sprinkle a combination of freshly grated Parmesan and Romano cheeses on top.
17. Bake for 35 to 40 minutes at 475°F until the crust is brown and crispy.
Note: Two pizzas are typically made, with one containing no meat (using zucchini peppers, mushrooms, onions) for those who prefer a vegetarian option. One pizza usually serves four people, along with a side salad.
